vitals.location: Ibiza, Balearic Islands, Spain (Stage 1 from Ibiza Town, Stage 2 from Sant Josep de sa Talaia, TT from Sant Antoni de Portmany)
vitals.field_size: ~500 planned/authorized (DGT provisional reservation; no confirmed-entry count published)
vitals.start_format: Group starts (Stages 1-2), individual departures (final 4.4km time trial)
vitals.entry_fee: €140 standard (through Aug 31), €150 from Sept 1 (plus platform commission)
vitals.feed_zones: Stage 1: 2 (Es Cubells, Cala Molí). Stage 2: 3 (Sant Llorenç, Portinatx, Santa Agnès). None on the TT.
vitals.cutoff_time: No fixed pace cutoff published; broom-vehicle removal, scheduled finishes 13:00 (Stage 1) / 15:00 (Stage 2)

climate.description: Early-to-mid October in the Balearics, with roads open to normal traffic throughout the event.
challenges: Riding on open (non-closed) public roads shared with normal traffic

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 54. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 53. EDITORIAL WAVE 10 FLAG (2026-07-24): on-file 280km/2,000m was not a real single-day figure or a documented cumulative total for this event — the verified 2026 event is a 3-day, 171.4km/2,750m+ multi-stage format (two mass-start road stages plus a short time trial), materially shorter in single-day distance but denser in climbing per km than the old figures implied. Dimension scores below were inherited from the incorrect vitals and have not been re-evaluated — flagging for human rescoring. No numeric changes made this wave.

course_description.character: A three-day, multi-stage cicloturista around Ibiza rather than a single long ride — the on-file '280km' never matched a real single day or the organizer's own published cumulative total (171.4km across three riding days; climbing is at least 2,750m — the organizer has not published an elevation figure for the closing time trial). Stage 1 runs from Ibiza Town to Sant Antoni via Es Cubells and Cala Molí; Stage 2 runs from Sant Josep to Sant Antoni via Sant Llorenç, Portinatx and Santa Agnès; the event closes with a short 4.4km individual time trial. The two road stages average roughly 18 and 15 meters of climbing per kilometer — dense, repeated punchy climbing rather than one defining summit. Roads stay open to normal traffic throughout, and only timed sectors plus the final TT determine classification; most of the riding is social group touring through Ibiza's five municipalities.
course_description.signature_challenge: The closing individual time trial in Sant Antoni de Portmany, following two days of dense, punchy road-stage climbing — the only fully timed stage; Stages 1-2 contain timed sectors but are not fully raced.
suffering_zones: Camí de Cas Coll and Cala Vedella (Stage 1 timed sectors)
The Cala Sant Vicent ascent and Ses Marrades (Stage 2 timed features)
Riding on open public roads shared with normal traffic across all three days

history.origin_story: Project created by Bartolo Planells in 2002; first held edition ran in 2004 with roughly 200 riders across three routes spanning Ibiza and Formentera. Now organized by Club Esportiu Ibiza Sport (also referenced as Club Esportiu IbizaSport / Ibiza Bike S.C.P.), with Bartolo's brother Juanjo Planells as the current organizer. The 2026 edition is the XXIII (23rd).
notable_moments: Featured a dedicated women's/adapted-cycling category in 2021, drawing over 360 participants and honoring Paralympic medalist Ricardo Ten
history.reputation: A long-running (23-edition), family-run Balearic cicloturista — more social touring event with a competitive time-trial finale than a fully raced gran fondo.

biased_opinion.verdict: 23 Editions Since 2004 — Just Not the 280km Ride On File
biased_opinion.summary: Vuelta Cicloturista a Ibiza Campagnolo is a 23-edition, family-organized event on Ibiza — but this profile's 280km/2,000m vitals never matched any real single day or documented total. The actual 2026 format is a three-day, 171.4km cumulative cicloturista: two social group-ride stages with timed climbing sectors, closed out by a short competitive time trial. It's dense, punchy island climbing (roughly 18m/km on Stage 1) over a three-day format, and roads stay open to normal traffic throughout — riders share the road, they don't get it closed for them. The organizer's own 'provisional' regulations misnumber the edition count and retain a leftover 2025 image-rights reference, and Juanjo Planells has publicly apologized for the disruption a past three-day edition caused residents.
strengths: Long-running (23 editions since 2004) and family-organized, founded by Bartolo Planells, now run by his brother Juanjo Planells
Dense, punchy climbing across two road stages plus a competitive time-trial finale
Tours five Ibiza municipalities with a documented history of inclusive programming (2021 adapted-cycling/women's category, 360+ participants)
Multi-day format for traveling riders who want to combine racing with an island trip
weaknesses: On-file 280km/2,000m never matched reality — actual cumulative distance is 171.4km, materially different from the former on-file figures
Roads are open to normal traffic throughout, not closed — a safety consideration the regulations themselves flag
Documented resident/traffic disruption complaints, with a public apology from the organizer for a past edition
Official provisional regulations misnumber the edition count and retain a leftover 2025 image-rights reference — verify details against the live event page
Strict late-withdrawal terms: 50% refund Sept 1-15, no refund after Sept 16
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A long-running Balearic cicloturista worth considering if you want punchy island climbing plus a time-trial finish over a social three-day format — just go in expecting 171km cumulative, not a 280km ride, and know the roads aren't closed.

final_verdict.one_liner: A 23-edition, three-day Ibiza cicloturista with punchy climbing and a time-trial finale — correcting a badly overstated 280km on-file distance.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes, if you want a social multi-day island cycling trip with real climbing and a competitive TT finish. Don't expect a 280km single-day distance, and be ready to share open roads with traffic.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a bigger single-day Balearic gran fondo: Mallorca 312 (312/226/167km, Playa de Muro, April). For another Mallorca multi-stage option: research the current status of Cinturón Ciclista Internacional a Mallorca before assuming it still runs as a stage race.

videos.title: XVIII edición de la Vuelta cicloturista Ibiza Campagnolo 2021
videos.description:
La XVIII edición de la cicloturista Ibiza Campagnolo ya se ha celebrado y aquí podéis disfrutar del resumen de sus cuatro etapas. 

Esta, ha sido una edición dedicada a las mujeres ciclistas y a la categoría de ciclismo adaptado, homenaje al medallista olímpico Ricardo Ten, que ha alcanzado su propio récord con más de 360 participantes.

Nuria Tauler y Marc Torres se han proclamado campeones absolutos de la Vuelta Cicloturista a Ibiza Campagnolo 2021 tras la disputa de la cuarta y última etapa, una contrarreloj individual por las calles de Sant Antoni que se adjudicaron Sandra Alonso y el ciclista ibicenco.

eligibility.notes: XXIII (23rd) edition confirmed for October 9-12, 2026 (Oct 9 registration/presentation, riding Oct 10-12), live registration open via RockTheSport at €140 (rising to €150 Sept 1). Confirmed genuinely distinct from Mallorca-island events (Mallorca 312, Cinturón Ciclista Internacional a Mallorca) in this database — no conflation. via codex gpt-5.6-sol web research, editorial wave 10.

catalog_flags.status_note: On-file vitals (280km/2,000m) corrected to the organizer's documented 2026 format: 171.4km cumulative over 3 riding days, 2,750m+ climbing. Taxonomy note: the event's own official FAQ describes it as a multi-stage format (two mass-start road stages plus an individual time trial), which may fit the corpus's `multi_stage` discipline better than `gran_fondo` — flagging for human taxonomy review, discipline enum left unchanged this wave.
